title: Electric Power Generation by Plant and Use for Selected Years, Iran 1968-1972 (incl. domestic/residential consumption by regional company)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank); underlying data from Ministry of Water and Power (Iran)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION. Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1974_economic_development_vol4_statistical_appendix.pdf, Table 15.3, page 138 (PDF page 146). Rendered to PNG via pdftoppm -r 200 and visually read. No value interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
units: unit not explicitly printed on the source page -- inferred to be million kWh (GWh) from context and cross-checked plausible against Iran's independently-known total generation scale c.1971 (~7.2 TWh, i.e. Grand Total row 7,244 matches if units are million kWh). Flagged explicitly as inferred, not fabricated.
notes: Breaks total electric power generation/consumption into Industrial, Agricultural, Street Lighting, Domestic, and Public/Commercial/Other use categories, both by individual regional electric company and nationally aggregated. The 'domestic' (residential) row -- 642 (1968) / 808 (1970) / 983 (1971) / 1,206 (1972) -- is this project's first direct measure of Iran's household/residential electricity consumption specifically (as opposed to WDI's economy-wide 'electric power consumption kWh per capita', which is not sector-disaggregated and only starts in data/processed/macro_wdi.csv from 1990). Can be divided by contemporaneous population (see macro_maddison.csv or iran_data_portal series) to derive a residential per-capita kWh figure for the early 1970s -- left as a derived-field exercise for data/processed/, not computed here since raw data must stay exactly as transcribed.
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription with visual verification, pdftoppm -r 200 render + Read tool)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
